Name: Fog
Description: Controls global atmospheric fog applied based on distance and height. Combines distance-based attenuation with height-dependent color blending to simulate realistic haze, mist, and atmospheric perspective.

Fields.Name: Color
Fields.Description: Base fog color applied over distance. Represents the primary atmospheric tint seen at far distances.

Fields.Name: Height Color
Fields.Description: Fog color used for height-based blending. Typically differs from base color to simulate ground fog or atmospheric layering.

Fields.Name: Distance
Fields.Description: Controls how quickly fog accumulates with distance from the camera. Higher values result in denser fog over shorter distances.

Fields.Name: Height Distance
Fields.Description: Controls how fog density changes with height. Determines how quickly fog fades or intensifies vertically.

Fields.Name: Distance Offset
Fields.Description: Offset applied to distance-based fog calculation, effectively shifting where fog begins relative to the camera.

Fields.Name: Height Offset
Fields.Description: Vertical offset applied to height-based fog calculations, allowing control over the base altitude of fog layers.

Fields.Name: Height Fog Intensity
Fields.Description: Strength of the height-based fog contribution. Higher values emphasize vertical fog layering effects.

Fields.Name: Fog Alpha
Fields.Description: Overall opacity of the fog effect. Controls how strongly fog blends over the scene.

Fields.Name: Fog Contrast
Fields.Description: Adjusts contrast of the fog blending curve. Higher values create sharper transitions between clear and foggy regions.
